preview.summary: Two long-awaited returnees open Group C, with Haiti back at a World Cup for the first time since 1974 and Scotland ending a 28-year absence. Both arrive in good qualifying form and know points here are vital in a group also containing Brazil and Morocco. Expect Scotland to control possession against an organised, counter-attacking Haiti.
preview.homeForm: Haiti topped CONCACAF Group C to qualify and have won three of their last five; ranked 83rd by FIFA.
preview.awayForm: Scotland won UEFA Group C with four wins, scoring 13 goals to qualify directly for the first time since 1982.
preview.homeKey: Jean-Ricner Bellegarde, Wolves midfielder driving Haiti's attack.
preview.awayKey: Scott McTominay, Napoli's box-crashing midfielder and qualifying joint-top scorer.
storylines: Haiti's first World Cup match in 52 years
Steve Clarke's Scotland chasing a rare tournament win
Caribbean flair versus Scottish midfield steel
preview.h2h: The two nations have no competitive history, making this a first World Cup meeting.
